The '''[[Star Wars: Episode II Attack of the Clones|''Star Wars'': Episode II ''Attack of the Clones'']] soundtrack''' was released on [[April 23]], [[2002]] by [[Sony]] Classical. The music was composed and conducted by [[John Williams]], and performed by the [[London Voices]] and [[London Symphony Orchestra]] in January 2002, with orchestrations provided by [[Conrad Pope]] and Eddie Karam. Shawn Murphy recorded and mixed the score. Peter Myles and [[Kenneth Wannberg]] served as music editors. Williams himself produced the recording sessions.

Four different soundtrack covers, each sold separately, were distributed at the time, one featuring [[Yoda]], another featuring [[Anakin Skywalker|Anakin]] and [[Padmé Amidala|Padmé]], a third featuring [[Jango Fett]], and the fourth featuring the film's final poster art.<ref name="SWArchive-Across the Stars Music Video to Debut">{{SWArchive|url=episode-ii/release/media/news20020419.html|text="Across the Stars" Music Video to Debut}}</ref><ref>{{WebCite|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=XhAEAAAAMBAJ|text=Master P the new no limit 10th anniversary|date=2002-03-16|archivedate=20230918024355|magazine=Billboard|volume=114|issue=11|page=9}}</ref>

For a limited time, a [[wikipedia:Walmart|WAL-MART]] retail exclusive version of the CD had bonus enhanced multimedia CD-ROM content when inserted into a computer featuring a link to an exclusive PC screensaver. A [[Wikipedia:Target Corporation|Target]]-exclusive edition featured the bonus track "On The Conveyor Belt."<ref name="soundtrack">{{WebCite|url=https://www.soundtrack.net/album/star-wars-episode-ii-attack-of-the-clones|text=Star Wars: Episode II - Attack of the Clones Soundtrack|author=Dan Goldwasser2004-06-21|work=Soundtrack.Net|archivedate=20230622064122}}</ref> [[wikipedia:Best Buy|Best Buy]] versions of the album included a limited edition Episode II Trading Card.<ref>{{WebCite|url=http://www.bestbuy.com/mandm/ProductInfo.asp?ST=11120810&m=254|text=Star Wars Episode 2: Attack Of The Clones Original Soundtrack|archivedate=20020805145305|agency=Best Buy|nolive=1}}</ref>

A remastered version of the soundtrack was released by [[Wikipedia:Walt Disney Records|Walt Disney Records]] on May 4, 2018.<ref name=":1">{{WebCite|publication-place=Burbank, California|url=https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/disney-music-group-set-to-release-first-6-remastered-star-wars-original-motion-picture-soundtracks-on-may-4-300617275.html|text=Disney Music Group Set To Release First 6 Remastered Star Wars Original Motion Picture Soundtracks On May 4|date=2018-03-21|archivedate=20230102013031|agency=PR Newswire}}</ref> supervised by [[Wikipedia: Shawn Murphy (sound engineer)|Shawn Murphy]] and [[Skywalker Sound]], this version was transferred to a new hi-resolution (24/192).<ref name="SW-Remasters of the First 6 Star Wars Soundtracks Coming May 4">{{SW|url=news/remasters-of-the-first-6-star-wars-soundtracks-coming-may-4|text=Remasters of the First 6 ''Star Wars'' Soundtracks Coming May 4}}</ref> This remaster was newly assembled from the highest-quality tapes available, rather than sourced from the existing 2002 album masters.<ref name="disney marketing video on remasters">{{OfficialYouTube|channel=@DisneyMusicVEVO|name=Disney Music VEVO|video=WSOzvCtaHmg|text=John Williams - Star Wars: Remastering The Score|archivedate=20220627150647}}</ref>

Due to the editing in the film, several effects were used to aid in transitions between edited cues. Several moments use a chime sound such as the transition from the [[Obi-Wan Kenobi|Obi-Wan]] [[Kamino]] moments to the Anakin/Padmé [[Naboo]] scenes. The Arena, for example, is almost completely tracked out and instead is replaced with "source" crowd sounds such as drums.

Once the [[First Battle of Geonosis/Legends|Battle of Geonosis]] begins, the editing becomes extensive, pulling from both [[Star Wars: Episode I The Phantom Menace|''The Phantom Menace'']] and [[Star Wars: Episode II Attack of the Clones|''Attack of the Clones'']], utilizing all forms of editing and supplementing transitions with timpani rolls. This was originally planned by [[John Williams]] and [[George Lucas]].

==Contents==
#[[Star Wars Main Title]] and Ambush on Coruscant – 3:46 {{Audio|MainTitle-TPM.flac|Listen}}
#[[Across the Stars]] (Love Theme from ''Attack of the Clones'') – 5:33 {{Audio|Across The Stars clip.flac|Listen}}
#Zam The Assassin and The Chase Through Coruscant – 11:07
#Yoda and the Younglings – 3:55
#Departing Coruscant – 1:44
#Anakin and Padmé – 3:57
#[[Jango's Escape]] – 3:48
#[[The Meadow Picnic]] – 4:14
#Bounty Hunter's Pursuit – 3:23
#Return to Tatooine – 6:57
#The Tusken Camp and The Homestead – 5:54
#Love Pledge and [[The Arena]] – 8:29 {{Audio|Arena_Theme.flac|Listen}}
#Confrontation with Count Dooku and Finale – 10:45
#On the Conveyor Belt – 3:02 {{C|Target exclusive bonus track}}

For the US release, early album prints titled track 2 as only "Love Theme from ''Attack of the Clones''" on the album's back and insert. Later releases re-named the track to "Across the Stars (Love Theme from ''Attack of the Clones'')". This was not the case for the international versions.

The Target-exclusive bonus track, "On the Conveyor Belt", is not included on the 2018 release by Walt Disney Records.

==Score information==
Little is known about the score. Due to the unpopularity of the two-disc ''Phantom Menace'' Ultimate Edition, no attempt was made to release more of the score.

The CD track "Zam The Assassin And The Chase Through Coruscant" contains the only appearance of an electric guitar in a ''Star Wars'' movie soundtrack. The director however did not like the guitar and chose not to feature it in the film.
